title: Elevator Pitch
html:
Elevator Pitch

The "have-an-elevator-pitch-for-yourself" practice — first surfaced in the YEBW debrief and carried for 7 years as a recurring self-instruction across Yahir's investor outreach, scholarship apps, and product framings.

Chronology

  • 2019-08-12 — 2019 08 12 keep things simple — canonical origin: post-YEBW debrief includes "Have an elevator pitch for your self."
  • 2021-11-15 — 2021 11 15 numbers in investors pitch — refined: numbers belong in the pitch, not the appendix
  • 2022-05-09 — 2022 05 09 Values Waht They Are Why They Are Imporant — values-first pitch framing
  • 2025-02-26 — 2025 02 26 screenshot 2025 02 26 at 2 14 45 pm a7961c54 — pitch-deck or app-onboarding screenshot
  • 2025-12-30 — 2025 12 30 direct offer — direct-offer mode — the pitch as commercial proposition
  • 2026-04-25 — 2026 04 25 cv cohort application — most recent: pitch as cohort-application copy

Yahir's pitch frame

  • Bidirectional: pitching is "like dating" — both sides are evaluating
  • First minute is the whole game: knowing the opening 60 seconds cold matters more than the deck
  • Numbers go in front, not at the end
  • Values-first: a pitch without a "why" loses to a pitch with one

Why it matters

The elevator-pitch isn't just a comms artifact — it's the format Yahir keeps re-using to summarize a project to himself. Every Brotein App Store description rewrite, every Rankett meetup prep, every CV cohort application is the same primitive: write the 60-second version, rewrite it 12 times, then operate from it. The 2019 YEBW debrief installs this as a permanent reflex.

Connections

yebw · asking the right question · piercing prompts · debt of gratitude · college admissions · rankett · brotein · the austin thesis
